String manipulation and conversion functions for memory and character arrays.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of standard library functions for memory and string manipulation, as well as functions for converting strings to integers. It includes functions such as [`memcmp`](<#memcmp>), [`memcpy`](<#memcpy>), [`memmove`](<#memmove>), and [`memset`](<#memset>) for handling memory operations. String manipulation functions like [`strcat`](<#strcat>), [`strncat`](<#strncat>), [`strlen`](<#strlen>), [`strnlen`](<#strnlen>), [`strcpy`](<#strcpy>), [`strncpy`](<#strncpy>), [`strcmp`](<#strcmp>), [`strncmp`](<#strncmp>), [`strchr`](<#strchr>), [`strrchr`](<#strrchr>), [`strcspn`](<#strcspn>), [`strspn`](<#strspn>), and [`strstr`](<#strstr>) are also declared, which provide various operations for concatenating, copying, comparing, and searching within strings. Additionally, the file declares conversion functions [`atoi`](<#atoi>), [`atol`](<#atol>), and [`strtol`](<#strtol>) for converting strings to integer values. The file includes the `<stddef.h>` header to ensure the availability of the `size_t` type and the `NULL` macro.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stddef.h`
